question: In Valentina Re’s contribution to the 2017 book “World Building: Transmedia, Fans, Industries”, what horror movie does the author cite as having popularized metalepsis between a dream world and reality? Use the complete name with article if any.

research_queries: "World Building: Transmedia, Fans, Industries" 2017 book Valentina Re
Valentina Re metalepsis horror movie dream world reality
"World Building Transmedia Fans Industries" 2017 table of contents chapters
Valentina Re horror film metalepsis between dream reality
"World Building: Transmedia, Fans, Industries" Valentina Re contribution chapter
working_plan: Search for the 2017 book 'World Building: Transmedia, Fans, Industries' to identify its structure and contributors
Locate Valentina Re's specific chapter or contribution within this book
Find the full text or detailed summary of Re's chapter that discusses horror movies and metalepsis
Identify the specific horror movie cited as popularizing metalepsis between dream world and reality
Verify the complete movie title including any articles (The, A, etc.) as specified in the question

solver.reasoning_summary: Found Valentina Re's chapter 'The Monster at the End of This Book: Metalepsis, Fandom, and World Making in Contemporary TV Series' from the 2017 book 'World Building: Transmedia, Fans, Industries'. In her discussion of the TV series Supernatural episode 'Dream a Little Dream of Me', she explicitly states that the episode 'proposes the metalepsis made popular by A Nightmare on Elm Street (Wes Craven, 1984), with the tragic consequences produced by the unexpected permeability between the dream world and the "real" world.' The complete movie title with article is 'A Nightmare on Elm Street'.
